title: Firmware-wide date/time provider abstraction
description: Why this exists: date/time is firmware-wide, not specific to UI or any one app channel. LICHEN firmware targets may get time from UI/local-client channels, internal RTC or monotonic clocks, onboard or external GNSS, gpsd bridges, SNTP/NTP, network-provided time, or static/manual configuration. Firmware needs one source-agnostic time service so scheduling, logs, security freshness, beacons, position timestamps, UI, CoAP resources, and app compatibility layers do not each invent their own time assumptions. What needs to be done: define and implement a firmware-wide date/time provider abstraction with source identification, source class (internal RTC/monotonic, GNSS, gpsd, SNTP/NTP, network, local UI/app/client, manual/static), wall-clock validity, monotonic time linkage, freshness/age, accuracy/stratum or quality metadata when available, trust/override policy, and conflict handling across hardware, network, and app-provided sources. Hardware/network/UI/app sources must feed this provider directly; UI and app interfaces may feed or consume it but do not own it. Acceptance criteria: supported time source classes are enumerated; precedence/override policy is documented; stale/fresh and valid/invalid wall-clock semantics are tested; SNTP/gpsd/GNSS/manual/app-injected sources use the same internal representation; UI consumes only unified provider state; CoAP/status resources expose source class, source name, wall-clock validity, age, and accuracy/quality where available; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes:
Related but separate from project-LICHEN-zte2 location provider: GNSS/gpsd may supply both time and position, but firmware should model time validity/accuracy/source independently from location fix/source.
Implemented no-hardware scope for firmware-wide time provider abstraction. Added HAL time source classes and rejection reasons, deterministic build/provision epoch floors, source precedence (manual/local/network/GNSS/RTC), freshness/age handling, explicit wall-clock validity, stale diagnostic retention, and GNSS location-time separation. Added app_interface time snapshot/submit APIs, HAL bridge mappings, gateway status CBOR wall-clock fields, and Meshtastic local-info wall-clock timestamp fallback while preserving legacy time_provider semantics. Created child project-LICHEN-2067.1 for SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH/release epoch build integration; user noted build-time stamps in firmware or board metadata are acceptable as the GNSS epoch source, but production metadata must be deterministic/tested. Reviews: initial independent reviews found stale diagnostic retention, legacy time_provider, Meshtastic timestamp fallback, provision diagnostics, app snapshot coverage, and build metadata follow-up gaps; all P1/P2 issues were fixed or filed. Final independent reviews Helmholtz/Lorentz/Hilbert found no P0/P1/P2 issues; all ran git diff --check and targeted diff inspections. Validation on EC2 i-05e27e074db786e22 using /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-time-provider: git diff --check passed locally. Focused Twister command: west twister -T lichen/tests/hal -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/gateway_status -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-time-provider-v6 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-time-provider/lichen ; result PASS 8/10 configs, 2 static skips, 143 cases, 51 skipped, 0 warnings, 168.63s. Broader consumer Twister command: west twister -T lichen/tests/hal -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/gateway_status -T lichen/tests/gateway_config -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -T lichen/tests/puck_location -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-time-provider-consumers-v3 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-time-provider/lichen ; result PASS 12/14 configs, 2 static skips, 185 cases, 51 skipped, 0 warnings, 248.36s. qemu gateway build command: west build -p always -b qemu_x86 lichen/apps/gateway -d build-gateway-qemu-time-provider-v3 -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-time-provider/lichen ; result PASS, RAM 246716 B / 31 MB (0.76%), IDT 0/2 KB. Known qemu warnings only: CONFIG_TIMER_RANDOM_GENERATOR test-only, no sources for drivers__entropy and drivers__spi. No hardware required for completed scope.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware firmware-wide time provider abstraction. Implemented HAL/app/gateway/Meshtastic integration, native_sim/qemu Twister validation, qemu gateway build evidence, and three clean final codereviews. Deferred build metadata wiring to child project-LICHEN-2067.1.
